content:
Member-only story

3 min read
Just now

--

Building software that serves a few hundred users is straightforward. Building software that reliably serves millions of users is a completely different engineering problem.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image used under fair use. Sourced from Google. Not owned by the author.At scale, success is rarely about using a particular framework or cloud provider. It comes from designing systems that remain available, resilient, and performant even when traffic grows unexpectedly or individual components fail.
This article covers the core engineering principles behind modern distributed systems.
1. Start with Stateless Services
A stateless service does not store user session or request-specific data in memory.
Every request contains everything required for processing, allowing any instance to handle it.
Load Balancer
|
-----------------------------
| | |
Service-1 Service-2 Service-3Benefits:

• Horizontal scaling becomes trivial.

• Failed instances can be replaced instantly.

• Traffic can be distributed evenly across replicas.
